properties: A launcher returns a run ID while a detached worker owns the complete plan.
Atomic state checkpoints and an ignored output log allow status, follow, and cancel to reconnect after a terminal or agent transport interruption.
Every child operation uses the existing shell-free watchdog with a 30-second heartbeat and a mandatory step deadline; each profile also has a total deadline.
Every production child has a 60–300 second real-output stall clamp in addition to its absolute deadline.
The package profile requests normal Syzygy shutdown, waits for app and owned llama-server exit, and limits forced recovery to captured Syzygy-owned process IDs.
An unrelated llama-server is never killed automatically.
Packaging fails unless build output proves Compiling app v and the packaged MCP smoke harness passes.
fixtures: Detached success survives launcher exit and records both completed checkpoints.
A nonzero child stops the plan and leaves the later step queued.
A hung child exits through watchdog code 124 and does not advance.
Explicit cancellation terminates the detached worker tree and records a terminal cancelled checkpoint.
A heartbeat-only child is terminated by stall exit 125 before its absolute deadline and does not advance.
remainingEvidence: Exercise scoped forced recovery against a deliberately unresponsive packaged GUI without unrelated llama-server processes.

checks: Tauri installer with Compiling app v
